content:
<system_instruction>
You are working inside Conductor, a Mac app that lets the user run many coding agents in parallel.
Your work should take place in the /Users/fcamblor/conductor/workspaces/mac-ai-trackers/managua directory (unless otherwise directed), which has been set up for you to work in.
Each workspace has a .context directory (gitignored) where you can save files to collaborate with other agents.
The target branch for this workspace is origin/main. Use this for actions like diffing (`git diff origin/main...`) or creating PRs (`gh pr create --base main`).


By default, the user will only see the last message that you send before stopping. Include all essential information in the last message. The intermediate messages will be collapsed and accessible by the user but not displayed by default.

If the user asks you to work on several unrelated tasks, you can suggest they start new workspaces.
Sometimes the user might send you a message they meant to send in a different workspace or a different chat. If something doesn't make sense in the context of your work, just ask. 
If the user asks for help with Conductor, you can ask them to go to "Help -> Send Feedback" to get in touch with our team.
</system_instruction>


<system-instruction>
To do immediately:

Rename the branch using `git branch -m` as soon as possible. Do not check the current branch name.

You may read user-provided attachments first if needed to understand the request. Otherwise, do this before using any skills or any other tools.

Choosing a branch name: use concrete, specific language, avoid abstract nouns, and be concise (<30 characters). Do not include any prefix in the name.

</system-instruction>

addedBlocks:
## claude-in-chrome
**IMPORTANT: Before using any chrome browser tools, you MUST first load them using ToolSearch.**

Chrome browser tools are MCP tools that require loading before use. Before calling any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tool:
1. Use ToolSearch with `select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__<tool_name>` to load the specific tool
2. Then call the tool

For example, to get tab context:
1. First: ToolSearch with query "select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp"
2. Then: Call mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp

## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## plugin:serena:serena
CRITICAL: Before starting to work on a coding task, call the `initial_instructions` tool to read the 'Serena Instructions Manual'.

## vibe_kanban
A Vibe Kanban MCP server for task, issue, repository, workspace, and session management. Use list/read tools first when you need IDs or current state. TOOLS: 'add_issue_tag', 'assign_issue', 'create_issue', 'create_issue_relationship', 'create_session', 'delete_issue', 'delete_issue_relationship', 'delete_workspace', 'get_execution', 'get_issue', 'get_repo', 'link_workspace_issue', 'list_issue_assignees', 'list_issue_priorities', 'list_issue_tags', 'list_issues', 'list_org_members', 'list_organizations', 'list_projects', 'list_repos', 'list_sessions', 'list_tags', 'list_workspaces', 'remove_issue_tag', 'run_session_prompt', 'start_workspace', 'unassign_issue', 'update_cleanup_script', 'update_dev_server_script', 'update_issue', 'update_session', 'update_setup_script', 'update_workspace'.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

content.thinking: The user wants to improve the hover legend on graphs to display both date AND HH:mm time, not just the date. Let me first rename the branch, then explore the codebase to find the relevant chart/graph code.

content.text: Je commence par renommer la branche, puis j'explore le code des graphes.

input.prompt: Dans le projet Swift macOS situé dans /Users/fcamblor/conductor/workspaces/mac-ai-trackers/managua, trouve le code qui gère l'affichage de la légende au survol (hover) sur les graphes/charts. Je cherche spécifiquement où le formatage de la date est fait pour le tooltip/légende au hover des graphiques. Retourne les fichiers et numéros de ligne pertinents. Cherche des termes comme: legend, hover, tooltip, date format, chart, axis, label dans les fichiers Swift et SwiftUI.

toolUseResult.prompt: Dans le projet Swift macOS situé dans /Users/fcamblor/conductor/workspaces/mac-ai-trackers/managua, trouve le code qui gère l'affichage de la légende au survol (hover) sur les graphes/charts. Je cherche spécifiquement où le formatage de la date est fait pour le tooltip/légende au hover des graphiques. Retourne les fichiers et numéros de ligne pertinents. content.thinking:
The issue is clear. For `thirtyDays` and `all` time windows, the format is `"MMM d"` which only shows the date without the time. The user wants to also show the time (`HH:mm`) for these windows too.

Let me look at the exact code to make the fix.

content.text: Le problème est clair : pour les fenêtres `.thirtyDays` et `.all`, le format est `"MMM d"` sans l'heure. Il faut ajouter `HH:mm`.

content.content: The file /Users/fcamblor/conductor/workspaces/mac-ai-trackers/managua/AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/Views/UsageHistoryChartView.swift has been updated successfully.

content.text: Le correctif est en place. Pour les fenêtres **30 jours** et **Tout**, la légende au hover affichera désormais `MMM d, HH:mm` (ex : `Apr 28, 14:35`) au lieu du simple `MMM d` — ce qui était déjà le cas pour la fenêtre 7 jours.

content.text:
# Smart commit

Tu dois committer les changements git en cours dans le repo courant en suivant les guidelines ci-dessous.

## Contexte utilisateur

Intent fourni par l'utilisateur (peut être vide) : ``

## Étapes

### 1. Analyser l'état du repo

Lance en parallèle :
- `git status` (sans `-uall`)
- `git diff --staged` et `git diff` pour voir les changements
- `git log -n 5 --pretty=format:'%h %s%n%b%n---'` pour voir les 4-5 derniers commits

### 2. Détecter les conventions du projet

À partir des 4-5 derniers commits :
- **Conventional commits ?** Si la majorité suit le format `type(scope): message` (feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test, perf, build, ci...) → utilise ce format. Sinon → format libre.
- **Langue ?** Détecte la langue dominante des messages (français, anglais, autre) → utilise la même langue. En cas de doute, français.
- **Style ?** Impératif vs descriptif, majuscule initiale ou non, présence de scope, etc. → imite.

### 3. Détecter un éventuel fixup

Si l'intent utilisateur mentionne un correctif (mots-clés : "fix", "corrige", "répare", "bug", "issue", "problème", "régression", "broken"...) :

1. Identifie les fichiers modifiés (`git diff --name-only HEAD` + fichiers staged).
2. Pour chaque fichier, regarde `git log -n 10 --oneline -- <fichier>` pour voir quels commits récents l'ont touché.
3. Si **un seul commit récent** (dans les ~5 derniers, non encore pushé sur main/master partagé — vérifie avec `git log @{u}..HEAD` si upstream) est clairement à l'origine du code cassé, propose un **`git commit --fixup=<sha>`** au lieu d'un commit classique.
4. Si ambigu ou si le commit date d'avant `@{u}` (déjà pushé en amont partagé), reste sur un commit classique.

Ne fais **pas** de rebase autosquash automatiquement — laisse ce soin à l'utilisateur.

### 4. Rédiger le message

Règles impératives :
- **Le message explique le POURQUOI**, pas le QUOI. Le diff dit déjà ce qui change. Le message dit pourquoi ce changement existe (motivation, contrainte, problème résolu, contexte métier).
- Sujet court (≤72 caractères), en suivant la convention détectée.
- Si le pourquoi ne tient pas dans le sujet, ajoute un corps (ligne vide + explication). Sinon, pas de corps inutile.
- Pas de name-dropping de fichiers/fonctions dans le sujet — le diff suffit.
- Pas de phrases vides genre « Mise à jour du code » ou « Divers fixes ».

Si l'intent utilisateur est vide ou trop vague pour déduire le pourquoi, et que le diff ne le rend pas évident, **demande à l'utilisateur** avant de committer plutôt que d'inventer.

### 5. Staging et commit

- Si rien n'est staged, stage les fichiers pertinents **par nom** (jamais `git add -A` ni `git add .` pour éviter d'embarquer secrets/binaires).
- Ignore les fichiers suspects (`.env`, `credentials*`, clés privées, dumps volumineux). Préviens l'utilisateur si tu en vois.
- Crée le commit via HEREDOC pour préserver le formatage multiligne.
- **Ne jamais** utiliser `--no-verify`, `--amend` (sauf demande explicite), ni `--no-gpg-sign`.
- Si un hook pre-commit échoue : diagnostique la cause, corrige, re-stage, et crée un **nouveau** commit (pas d'amend).

### 6. Vérification finale

Après commit, lance `git status` et `git log -1` pour confirmer. Reporte brièvement : sha court + sujet + (fixup le cas échéant).

## Garde-fous

- Ne push jamais.
- Ne crée pas de commit vide.
- Ne touche pas à `git config`.
- Si plusieurs "unités logiques" distinctes sont mélangées dans le diff, préviens l'utilisateur et propose de splitter plutôt que de tout entasser. Tu peux utiliser `git stash` (avec `-u`/`--keep-index` si pertinent) pour isoler des sous-ensembles — mais cette commande n'est **pas** auto-approuvée, l'utilisateur devra confirmer à chaque usage : ne l'utilise que si c'est vraiment nécessaire.

cc6972f feat(menubar): hide minutes for time windows exceeding one day by default
When a metric's time-until-reset exceeds 24 hours, hiding the minutes
component (e.g., "6d 8h" instead of "6d 8h 13m") reduces visual clutter
in the menu bar. Enable this by default for new segments and backfill
existing configurations without the flag (migration via Codable fallback).

The screenshot and description were outdated after the display modes
feature shipped — neither mentioned the Consumed/Remaining toggle for
the usage percentage nor the "hide minutes when over 1 day" option for
the time-until-reset segment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: ef0691ee2a11

---
16b55ee fix(concurrency): avoid Sendable crossing in window close observer
The block-based NotificationCenter API marks its closure @Sendable,
causing Swift 6 to reject access to @MainActor-isolated NSWindow
properties even when queue: .main is specified. Switching to the
selector-based observer API lets the @MainActor-isolated method access
those properties without any concurrency violation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: 976c75bb3c5c

content.text:
Conventions détectées : **`type(scope): message`** en anglais (commits récents : `feat(menubar):`, `fix(concurrency):`, etc.). C'est une correction sur le formatage du hover legend des graphes.

Je stage et commit :

input.command:
git add AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/Views/UsageHistoryChartView.swift && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(chart): display date and time in hover legend for all time windows

The chart hover legend now consistently shows both date and time
(e.g. "Apr 28, 14:35") across all time windows, improving clarity
when hovering over data points. Previously, 6h/24h windows showed only
time, and 30d/all windows showed only date.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
